59	    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
60	)

70	)

file.content:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

content.text: This is a single, isolated file edit (adding a new type after `isbn`). Let me quickly check with agent2 whether they also need this file before editing.

input.command: coop-send agent2 --type "SURVEY" --files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --note "Adding new 'ssn' type (Regex) after the existing isbn definition, near line 70. Should be a small self-contained addition."

content.text: Same shared file. Need to assign a single owner. I'll claim it since I was first, and implement both additions.

input.command: coop-send agent2 --type "CLAIM" --files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --note "I'll own this file and implement both additions: 'ssn' (mine) and 'hash_sha256' (yours) as separate module-level Regex constants appended after isbn. Please send SPEC with exact regex pattern/name/comment you want for hash_sha256."

content.text: Both of us claimed simultaneously. I'll defer to agent2 to avoid conflict, and send my SPEC for the `ssn` type.

input.command: coop-send agent2 --type "DEFER" --files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --spec "Add after the existing isbn definition (currently ends around line 70): a blank line, then a short comment '# Matches a US Social Security Number in the format XXX-XX-XXXX' followed by: ssn = Regex(r\"(?!000|666|9\\d{2})\\d{3}-(?!00)\\d{2}-(?!0000)\\d{4}\"). This must validate SSNs of format 3 digits, hyphen, 2 digits, hyphen, 4 digits, excluding invalid area numbers 000/666/900-999, invalid group 00, and invalid serial 0000, consistent with real SSA rules. Must be importable as 'from outlines.types import ssn' and usable as a pydantic field type (like isbn is), e.g. class User(BaseModel): social_security_number: ssn matching '123-45-6789'." --note "ssn = Regex(...), place right after isbn block since you're owning this file. I will not edit outlines/types/__init__.py myself."

content.text: Agent2 acknowledged ownership and my SPEC. Since they own the only file this feature touches, I have no files left to implement myself. I'll wait for their DONE, then verify the result.

input.command: coop-send agent2 --type "DONE" --files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --note "Nothing further from me — this file is fully owned by you. Waiting for your DONE before I verify and write patch.txt."

content.text: Agent2 is done. Let me verify the actual file content before submitting.

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

content.text: Since agent2 owns `outlines/types/__init__.py` and confirmed they implemented the `ssn` type per my SPEC, I have no files left to edit myself (each agent works in an isolated copy, and the coordination protocol means I must not duplicate the edit). My patch should be empty — the feature is delivered via agent2's patch.

input.command: git diff > patch.txt && cat patch.txt && wc -l patch.txt

content.text:
Coordination with agent2 is complete. Since my feature only touched `outlines/types/__init__.py`, and that file was a shared conflict with agent2's `hash_sha256` addition, I deferred ownership to agent2 (per the cooperation protocol) after sending a precise SPEC for the `ssn` type:

```python
ssn = Regex(r"(?!000|666|9\d{2})\d{3}-(?!00)\d{2}-(?!0000)\d{4}")
```

placed right after the `isbn` definition, with a comment. Agent2 acknowledged, implemented it alongside their `hash_sha256` type, and confirmed verification (regex matches, pydantic model, JSON schema, existing tests all pass).

Per the protocol, I did not edit the file myself (avoiding a collision), so my own `patch.txt` is empty — the `ssn` feature is delivered in agent2's patch to `outlines/types/__init__.py`.
